{{short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Djibouti women's national football team}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Djibouti
| Badge              = 
| Nickname           = {{lang|ar|أسماك قرش البحر الأحمر}} <br> {{lang|fr|Requins de la Mer Rouge}} ([[Shark]]s of the [[Red Sea]])
| Association        = [[Djiboutian Football Federation|Fédération Djiboutienne de Football]] (FDF)
| Sub-confederation  = [[Council for East and Central Africa Football Associations|CECAFA]]<br>(East & Central Africa)
| Confederation      = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Coach              = [[Mohamed Meraneh Hassan]] (interim)
| Captain            = [[Mahdi Houssein Mahabeh]]
| Most caps          = [[Ali Youssouf Farada]] (39)
| Top scorer         = [[Samuel Akinbinu]] (10)
| Home Stadium       = [[Stade du Ville|El Hadj Hassan Gouled Aptidon Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme     = DJI
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|DJI}}
| FIFA max           = 169
| FIFA max date      = December 1994
| FIFA min           = 203
| FIFA min date      = April–July 2015, November 2015
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Djibouti}}
| Elo max            = 94
| Elo max date       = 1947
| Elo min            = 214
| Elo min date       = 2016
| pattern_la1        = _dji24h
| pattern_b1         = _dji24h
| pattern_ra1        = _dji24h
| pattern_sh1        = _dji24h 
| pattern_so1        = _dji24h
| leftarm1           = 
| body1              = 
| rightarm1          = 
| shorts1            = 
| socks1             = FFFFFF
| pattern_la2        = _dji24a
| pattern_b2         = _dji24a
| pattern_ra2        = _dji24a
| pattern_sh2        = _dji24a
| pattern_so2        = _dji24a
| socks2             = 45d23e
| First game         = '''{{fb|French Somaliland|1830}}''' 0–5 {{fb-rt|ETH|1897}}<br>([[French Somaliland]]; 5 December 1947)<br>''Post-independence''<br>{{fb|ETH|1975}} 8–1 {{fb-rt|DJI}}<br>([[Addis Ababa]], [[Ethiopia]]; 27 March 1983)
| Largest win        = {{fb|DJI}} 4–1 {{fb-rt|YMD}}<br>([[Djibouti (city)|Djibouti City]], [[Djibouti]]; 26 February 1988)<br>{{fb|DJI}} 3–0 {{fb-rt|Mauritius}}<br>([[Djibouti City]], [[Djibouti]]; 23 November 2019)
| Largest loss       = {{nowrap|{{fb|UGA}} 10–1 {{fb-rt|DJI}}<br>([[Kigali]], [[Rwanda]]; 9 December 2001)<br>{{fb|RWA}} 9–0 {{fb-rt|DJI}}<br>([[Dar es Salaam]], [[Tanzania]]; 13 December 2007)}}
|2ndRegional cup apps=12|2ndRegional name=[[CECAFA Cup]]|2ndRegional cup first=[[1994 CECAFA Cup|1994]]|2ndRegional cup best=Group stage ([[1994 CECAFA Cup|1994]], [[1999 CECAFA Cup|1999]], [[2000 CECAFA Cup|2000]], [[2001 CECAFA Cup|2001]], [[2005 CECAFA Cup|2005]], [[2006 CECAFA Cup|2006]], [[2007 CECAFA Cup|2007]], [[2008 CECAFA Cup|2008]], [[2009 CECAFA Cup|2009]], [[2011 CECAFA Cup|2011]], [[2015 CECAFA Cup|2015]], [[2019 CECAFA Cup|2019]])}}

The '''Djibouti national football team''' ({{langx|ar|منتخب جيبوتي لكرة القدم}}, {{langx|fr|Équipe de football de Djibouti}}), nicknamed the "[[Shark]]s of the [[Red Sea]]" ({{langx|ar|أسماك قرش البحر الأحمر}}, {{langx|fr|Requins de la Mer Rouge}}), is the national [[Association football|football]] team of [[Djibouti]]. It is controlled by the [[Djiboutian Football Federation]] and is a member of the [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF) and the [[Union of Arab Football Associations]] (UAFA). The Djibouti national football team's first win in a full [[FIFA]]-sanctioned international match was a 1–0 win vs. [[Somalia national football team|Somalia]] in the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF first round|first round]] of the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2010 FIFA World Cup qualification]].

==History==

=== French Somaliland (1947–1960) ===
{{Anchor|French Somaliland}}
Djibouti played its first international match under the name ''French Somaliland'', at home against neighbouring [[Ethiopia national football team|Ethiopia]] on 5 December 1947 and lost 5–0. This was also Ethiopia's debut.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablese/ethi-intres.html|title=Ethiopia – List of International Matches|website=RSSSF.com|publisher=Barrie Courtney and RSSSF|date=3 December 2011|access-date=6 December 2011|archive-date=4 March 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230304232705/https://www.rsssf.org/tablese/ethi-intres.html|url-status=live}}</ref> The two played again in Djibouti on 1 June 1948 and Ethiopia won 2–1. On 1 May 1949, the fixture was played for the ''Emperor Cup'' in Ethiopia, and the host won 6–0. In 1954, Djibouti played Ethiopia three times: a 10–2 away loss on 1 May, a 2–0 home loss on 1 June and a 2–1 home loss the day after. Djibouti did not play a match again until 1960, when it entered a tournament for French-speaking countries held in [[Madagascar]]. The team lost 9–2 in the first round to [[Cameroon national football team|Cameroon]] on 13 April. This was the squad's last game as French Somaliland.

=== Djibouti (1977–present) ===
After gaining independence in 1977, the team played under the name Djibouti for the first time against Ethiopia in an away match on 27 March 1983 and lost 8–1. The two played again two days later with Ethiopia again victorious, by 4–2. After a third friendly against Ethiopia, a 2–0 home defeat on 23 March 1984, Djibouti entered a tournament in Ethiopia against the host and [[Zimbabwe national football team|Zimbabwe]]. They lost 2–0 to Ethiopia on 3 June and then 3–1 to Zimbabwe on 7 June.

Djibouti's first appearance at the [[CECAFA Cup]], a local competition for nations in East and Central Africa, was in Kenya in 1994. These were its first matches since defeating South Yemen in 1988. The Djibouti squad lost 4–1 to the hosts on 28 November, 2–1 to [[Somalia national football team|Somalia]] on 1 December, and 3–0 to [[Tanzania national football team|Tanzania]] on 3 December. Djibouti did not advance to the next round.

After the 1994 CECAFA Cup, Djibouti did not play a match until the qualification campaign for the [[1998 African Cup of Nations]] in [[Burkina Faso]]. They were drawn in a two-legged qualifier against Kenya, and lost the first leg 3–0 away on 31 July 1998. The second leg at home was lost 9–1 on 15 August and Kenya went through 12–1 on aggregate.

In 1998, Djibouti became a member of the [[Union of Arab Football Associations]] (UAFA). The football squad has since participated in the [[Arab Games]], a regional [[multi-sport event]] held between nations from the [[Arab World]].

Djibouti entered its first ever World Cup qualification in an attempt to reach the [[2002 FIFA World Cup]] in [[South Korea]] and [[Japan]]. In Pool D of the first round of [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|African qualification]], it was drawn against the [[DR Congo national football team|DR Congo]] in a two-legged qualifying preliminary. Djibouti hosted the first leg at [[Stade du Ville]] in [[Djibouti (city)|Djibouti]] on 7 April 2000, drawing the match 1–1 before a crowd of 2,700 fans.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/archive/edition=4395/preliminaries/preliminary=3868/matches/match=19532/report.html |title=Djibouti – Congo DR |access-date=2010-10-21 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091012150241/http://www.fifa.com/worldcup/archive/edition%3D4395/preliminaries/preliminary%3D3868/matches/match%3D19532/report.html |archive-date=2009-10-12 }}</ref> The squad lost the second leg 9–1 away at the [[Stade des Martyrs]] in [[Kinshasa]] and the DR Congo advanced 10–2 on aggregate.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/archive/edition=4395/preliminaries/preliminary=3868/matches/match=19533/report.html |title=Congo DR – Djibouti |access-date=2010-10-21 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090425111124/http://www.fifa.com/worldcup/archive/edition%3D4395/preliminaries/preliminary%3D3868/matches/match%3D19533/report.html |archive-date=2009-04-25 }}</ref>

Djibouti has never played in the [[African Cup of Nations]], with the team regularly withdrawing or not entering for financial reasons.

Prior to their four preliminary qualifiers in late 2019, Djibouti had 2 wins, 3 draws and 55 defeats from 60 competitive matches. However, a number of new players were called up and results finally improved. First, in the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification]], Djibouti beat [[Eswatini national football team|Eswatini]] 2–1 at home and drew 0–0 in [[Manzini, Eswatini|Manzini]] to advance to the second round for the first time since the 2010 qualifying when they beat Somalia 1-0 (2–1 on aggregate). This was a massive improvement from the previous edition when Djibouti had also played Eswatini and lost 8–1 on aggregate. One month later, Djibouti played two 1–1 draws against [[Gambia national football team|Gambia]] in the [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round]], only losing the tie on penalties.

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Djibouti national football team results}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

=== 2025 ===
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 5 September
| time       = {{UTZ|16:00|0}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|DJI}}
| score      = 0–6
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020770
| team2      = {{fb|BFA}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = 
*[[Cyriaque Irié|Irié]] {{goal|16}}
*[[Josué Tiendrébéogo|Tiendrébéogo]] {{goal|25}}
*[[Edmond Tapsoba|Tapsoba]] {{goal|36||43}}
*[[Dango Ouattara|Da. Ouattara]] {{goal|59||86|pen.}}
| stadium    = [[Estádio 24 de Setembro]]
| location   = [[Bissau]], Guinea-Bissau
| attendance = 
| referee    = Younoussa Tawel Camara ([[Guinean Football Federation|Guinea]])
| result     = L
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 8 September
| time       = {{UTZ|16:00|0}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|GNB}}
| score      = 2–0
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020769
| team2      = {{fb|DJI}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Iano Imbeni|Imbeni]] {{goal|54}}
*[[Fali Candé|Candé]] {{goal|82}}
| goals2     = 
| stadium    = [[Estádio 24 de Setembro]]
| location   = [[Bissau]], Guinea-Bissau
| attendance = 
| referee    = Aklesso Gnama ([[Togolese Football Federation|Togo]])
| result     = L
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 8 October
| time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|DJI}}
| score      = 0–3
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021032
| team2      = {{fb|EGY}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = 
*[[Ibrahim Adel|Adel]] {{goal|8}}
*[[Mohamed Salah|Salah]] {{goal|14||84}}
| stadium    = [[Larbi Zaouli Stadium]]
| location   = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
| attendance = 1,200
| referee    = Tanguy Mebiame ([[Gabonese Football Federation|Gabon]])
| result     = L
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 12 October
| time       = {{UTZ|20:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|DJI}}
| score      = 1–2
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021033
| team2      = {{fb|SLE}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Abdichakour Farah|Farah]] {{goal|29}}
| goals2     = 
*[[Juma Bah|Bah]] {{goal|45+2}}
*[[Sallieu Tarawallie|Tarawallie]] {{goal|72}}
| stadium    = [[Larbi Zaouli Stadium]]
| location   = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
| attendance = 
| referee    = Thulani Sibandze ([[Eswatini Football Association|Eswatini]])
| result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round= [[2025 FIFA Arab Cup qualification]]
|date= 26 November
|time= {{UTZ|16:00|3}}
|team1= {{fb-rt|BHR}}
|score= 1–0
|team2= {{fb|DJI}}
|report= https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/10013/290556/291903/400020250
|goals1= [[Mohamed Al-Romaihi|Al-Romaihi]] {{goal|36}}
|goals2=
|location= [[Al-Rayyan (city)|Al-Rayyan]], Qatar
|stadium= [[Jassim bin Hamad Stadium]]
|attendance=
|referee= [[Juan Gabriel Benítez]] ([[Paraguayan Football Association|Paraguay]])
|result= L
}}

===2026===
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round|2027 AFCON qualification]]
| date       = 26 March
| time       = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|DJI}}
| score      = 0–4
| report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-26/djibouti-vs-south-sudan/4048691
| team2      = {{fb|SSD}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = *[[Teng Kuol|Kuol]] {{Goal|31}}
*[[Bangasi Justin|Justin]] {{Goal|49}}
*[[Peter Maker|Maker]] {{Goal|66}}
*[[Keer Mangar|Mangar]] {{Goal|81}}
| stadium    = [[Juba Stadium]]
| location   = [[Juba]], South Sudan
| attendance = 0 ([[Behind closed doors (sport)|Behind closed doors]])<ref>{{cite web |last= |first= |date=20 March 2026|title=South Sudan to host Djibouti in AFCON qualifiers behind closed doors|url=https://www.sudanspost.com/south-sudan-to-host-djibouti-in-afcon-qualifiers-behind-closed-doors/|website=sudanspost.com|location=[[Juba]]|publisher=[[Sudans Post]]|access-date=22 March 2026}}</ref>
| referee    = [[Tewodros Mitiku]] ([[Ethiopian Football Federation|Ethiopia]])
| result     = L
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round|2027 AFCON qualification]]
| date       = 29 March
| time       = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|SSD}}
| score      = 0–1 
| report     = [https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-29/south-sudan-vs-djibouti/4048692 Report] 
| team2      = {{fb|DJI}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = *[[Samuel Akinbinu|Akinbinu]] {{Goal|77}} 
| stadium    = [[Juba Stadium]]
| location   = [[Juba]], South Sudan
| attendance = 0 ([[Behind closed doors (sport)|Behind closed doors]])
| referee    = Retṧelisitsoe Molise ([[Lesotho Football Association|Lesotho]]) 
| result     = W 
}}

==Coaches==
{| class="wikitable"
!Name
!Nat
!Period
!Matches
!Wins
!Draws
!Losses
!Win %
|-
| Mohamed Bader
| align="center" |{{flagicon|DJI}}
| align="center" |{{Circa|1998}} – Dec 2001
| align="center" |15
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |2
| align="center" |13
| align="center" |0.00%
|-
| Ahmed Hussein
| align="center" |{{flagicon|DJI}}
| align="center" |Oct 2007 – Dec 2007
| align="center" |4
| align="center" |1
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |3
| align="center" |25.00%
|-
| [[Mohamed Abar]]
| align="center" |{{flagicon|DJI}}
| align="center" |Jan 2008 – Jun 2008
| align="center" |4
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |4
| align="center" |0.00%
|-
| [[Ahmed Abdelmonem]]
| align="center" |{{flagicon|EGY}}
| align="center" |Jul 2008 – Jul 2010
| align="center" |11
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |1
| align="center" |10
| align="center" |0.00%
|-
| [[Noureddine Gharsalli]]
| align="center" |{{flagicon|TUN}}
| align="center" |Oct 2011 – Jul 2016
| align="center" |5
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |5
| align="center" |0.00%
|-
| [[Mike Gibson (footballer)|Michael Gibson]]<ref>{{cite web|title=Football Manager proves England and Wayne Rooney don't need Gareth Southgate to beat Malta|url=http://metro.co.uk/2016/10/08/football-manager-proves-england-and-wayne-rooney-dont-need-gareth-southgate-to-beat-malta-6179009/|website=Metro|access-date=5 April 2017|archive-date=21 May 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170521103644/http://metro.co.uk/2016/10/08/football-manager-proves-england-and-wayne-rooney-dont-need-gareth-southgate-to-beat-malta-6179009/|url-status=live}}</ref>
| align="center" |{{flagicon|ENG}}
| align="center" |Jul 2016 – Apr 2017
| align="center" |4
| align="center" |1
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |3
| align="center" |25.00%
|-
| [[Moussa Ghassoum]]
| align="center" |{{flagicon|MTN}}
|Dec 2017 – Apr 2019
| align="center" |5
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |5
| align="center" |0.00%
|-
| [[Julien Mette]]
| align="center" |{{flagicon|FRA}}
|Apr 2019 – Oct 2021 
| align="center" |13
| align="center" |3
| align="center" |3
| align="center" |7
| align="center" |23.08%
|-
| [[Mohamed Meraneh Hassan]]
| align="center" |{{flagicon|DJI}}
|Oct 2021 – Jan 2022
| align="center" |6
| align="center" |1
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |5
| align="center" |16.67%
|-
| [[Abdourahman Okie Hadi]] 
| align="center" |{{flagicon|DJI}}
|Jan 2022 – present
| align="center" |4
| align="center" |2
| align="center" |2
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |50.00%
|}


==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were selected for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A]] matches against [[Burkina Faso national football team|Burkina Faso]] and [[Ethiopia national football team|Ethiopia]] on 21 and 24 March 2025.<ref>{{cite web |title=Eliminatoires Coupe du Monde 2026 |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=973123044955384&set=a.575604238040602 |website=Facebook |publisher=Fédération Djiboutienne de Football |access-date=21 March 2025 |date=13 March 2025}}</ref>

''Caps and goals are correct as of 24 March 2025, after the match against [[Ethiopia national football team|Ethiopia]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=GK|name=[[Sulait Luyima]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1993|11|4}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[AS Arta/Solar7|Arta Solar 7]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=GK|name=[[Moktar Youssouf Said]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|2004|10|12}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FC Dikhil/SGDT|Dikhil]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Omar Mahamoud (footballer)|Omar Mahamoud]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|10|19|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AS Arta/Solar7|Arta Solar 7]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=DF|name=[[Ali Youssouf Farada]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1995|8|25}}|caps=38|goals=1|club=[[AS Port]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=DF|name=[[Yabe Siad Isman|Yabe Siad]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1998|03|12}}|caps=29|goals=1|club=[[AS Arta/Solar7|Arta Solar 7]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=DF|name=[[Moussa Hamadou Araita]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1997|07|24}}|caps=20|goals=0|club=[[AS Port]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=DF|name=[[Moustapha Abdi Osman]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1992|01|08}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Garde Républicaine FC|Garde Républicaine]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=DF|name=[[Ibrahim Idriss Mohamed]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|2002|11|14}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[AS Ali Sabieh/Djibouti Télécom|ASAS Djibouti Télécom]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=DF|name=[[Yonis Kireh]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|05|10|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Virginia Tech Hokies men's soccer|Virginia Tech]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=DF|name=[[Ahmed Zakaria]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|11|12|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[CF Gendarmerie Nationale|Gendarmerie Nationale]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=2|pos=DF|name=[[Aboubaker Liban Abdi]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|11|28|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=''Unknown''}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=MF|name=[[Doualeh Mahamoud Elabeh]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1991|11|11}}|caps=34|goals=1|club=[[AS Arta/Solar7|Arta Solar 7]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=5|pos=MF|name=[[Hamza Abdi Idleh]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1991|12|16}}|caps=30|goals=2|club=[[FC Dikhil/SGDT|Dikhil]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=MF|name=[[Warsama Hassan]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1999|03|17}}|caps=28|goals=2|club=[[FC AP Brera Strumica|AP Brera]]|clubnat=MKD}}
{{nat fs g player|no=7|pos=MF|name=[[Maarouf Abass Abaneh]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|11|8|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=''Unknown''}}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=MF|name=[[Sadik Aden]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|11|28|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=''Unknown''}}
{{nat fs g player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Ahemdini Ali Gohar]]|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=''Unknown''}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=FW|name=[[Mahdi Houssein Mahabeh]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1995|12|20}}|caps=32|goals=7|club=[[AS Arta/Solar7|Arta Solar 7]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=FW|name=[[Samuel Akinbinu]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1999|06|06}}|caps=23|goals=10|club=[[AS Arta/Solar7|Arta Solar 7]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=FW|name=[[Gabriel Dadzie]]|age={{bda|1997|3|6|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=6|club=[[Al Bashayer]]|clubnat=LBY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=FW|name=[[Ahmed Youssouf Omar]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1998|09|1}}|caps=16|goals=0|club=[[AS Port]]|clubnat=DJI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=FW|name=[[Moussa Amoud Wais]]|age= {{bda|2001|2|20|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=''Unknown''}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=FW|name=[[Mouad Amir Mahamed]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|11|28|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=''Unknown''}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following footballers were part of a national selection in the past 12 months, but are not part of the current squad.
<!--Sorted by position, most recent call-up, caps, goals and last name.-->
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Aboubaker Guedi]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1999|9|4}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[SDC Group/Hôpital Balbala FC|SDC Group]]|clubnat=DJI|latest=v. {{fb|ETH}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Warsama Ibrahim Aden]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1998|05|12}}|caps=23|goals=1|club=[[AS Port]]|clubnat=DJI|latest=v. {{fb|ETH}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Fouad Moussa Robleh]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1993|4|28}}|caps=15|goals=1|club=[[AS Arta/Solar7|Arta Solar 7]]|clubnat=DJI|latest=v. {{fb|ETH}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abdillahi Elmi]]|age={{bda|2003|3|19|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[AS Ali Sabieh/Djibouti Télécom|ASAS Djibouti Télécom]]|clubnat=DJI|latest=v. {{fb|ETH}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Djimaleh Awaleh Kayad]]|age={{bda|1995|10|20|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[CF Gendarmerie Nationale|Gendarmerie]]|clubnat=DJI|latest=v. {{fb|ETH}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ahmed Mohamed Aden]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1990|12|4}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[AS Arta/Solar7|Arta Solar 7]]|clubnat=DJI|latest=v. {{fb|ETH}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Awaleh Hoch Gedo]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|2001|10|18}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Garde Républicaine FC]]|clubnat=DJI|latest=v. {{fb|ETH}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ramadan Abdi Abdillahi]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1998|1|18}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[AS Ali Sabieh/Djibouti Télécom|ASAS Djibouti Télécom]]|clubnat=DJI|latest=v. {{fb|ETH}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Zakaria Abdi Mouhoumed]]|age={{bda|2000|4|23|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[SDC Group]]|clubnat=DJI|latest=v. {{fb|ETH}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mahad Abdi]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1999|7|11}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Garde Républicaine FC]]|clubnat=DJI|latest=v. {{fb|ETH}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Moktar Hassan]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1998|4|15}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[AS Ali Sabieh/Djibouti Télécom|ASAS Djibouti Télécom]]|clubnat=DJI|latest=v. {{fb|ETH}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|26 November 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Djibouti |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/de/country/52/Djibouti.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Djibouti.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
!class="unsortable" style="width:200px;"|Name
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
!class="unsortable" style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|align=left|'''[[Ali Youssouf Farada]]'''
|41
|1
|2017–present
|-
|2
|align=left|'''[[Doualeh Mahamoud Elabeh]]'''
|35
|1
|2016–present
|-
|3
|align=left|[[Daoud Wais]]
|34
|1
|2008–2021
|-
|4
|align=left|{{ill|Daher Mohamed Kadar|it}}
|33
|1
|2006–2017
|-
|rowspan=2|5
|align=left|'''[[Mahdi Houssein Mahabeh]]'''
|32
|7
|2016–present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Yabe Siad]]'''
|32
|1
|2019–present
|-
|7
|align=left|'''[[Warsama Hassan]]'''
|31
|2
|2019–present
|-
|8
|align=left|'''[[Hamza Abdi Idleh]]'''
|30
|2
|2016–present
|-
|9
|align=left|[[Innocent Mbonihankuye]]
|25
|0
|2019–2023
|-
|rowspan=3|10
|align=left|'''[[Samuel Akinbinu]]'''
|23
|10
|2021–present
|-
|align=left|[[Guedi Hassan]]
|23
|0
|2007–2016
|-
|align=left|'''[[Ibrahim Aden Warsama]]'''
|23
|1
|2015–present
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===
[[File:Samuel Akinbinu 2021 (cropped).jpg|right|175px|thumb|Samuel Akinbinu is Djibouti's top scorer with 8 goals.]]

{|class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
!class="unsortable" width=200px|Name
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
!class="unsortable" width=100px|Career
|-
|1
|align="left"|'''[[Samuel Akinbinu]]'''
|10
|23
|{{#expr:10/23 round 2}}
|2021–present
|-
|2
|align="left"|'''[[Mahdi Houssein Mahabeh]]'''
|7
|32
|{{#expr:7/32 round 2}}
|2016–present
|-
|3
|align="left"|'''[[Gabriel Dadzie]]'''
|6
|18
|{{#expr:6/18 round 2}}
|2022–present
|-
|rowspan=2|4
|align="left"|[[Abdourahman Okieh Hadi]]
|3
|4
|{{#expr:3/4 round 2}}
|2005–2006
|-
|align="left"|[[Ahmed Daher]]
|3
|13
|{{#expr:3/13 round 2}}
|2007–2009
|-
|rowspan=5|6
|align="left"|[[Ahmed Daoud]]
|2
|3
|{{#expr:2/3 round 2}}
|2011
|-
|align="left"|[[Arid Ahmed Mohamed]]
|2
|5
|{{#expr:2/5 round 2}}
|1999–2000
|-
|align="left"|[[Mohamed Liban]]
|2
|17
|{{#expr:2/17 round 2}}
|2008–2015
|-
|align="left"|'''[[Hamza Abdi Idleh]]'''
|2
|30
|{{#expr:2/30 round 2}}
|2016–present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Warsama Hassan]]'''
|2
|31
|{{#expr:2/31 round 2}}
|2019–present
|-
|}

==Competition records==
{{col-begin}}
{{col-2}}

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 0
!colspan=6|Appearances: 6
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|colspan=8|''Part of {{fb|France}}''
|colspan=6|''Part of {{fb|France}}''
|-
|[[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]] to [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''||colspan=8|''Declined participation''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2||0||1||1||2||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''||colspan=8|''Declined participation''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''
|7||1||0||6||3||30
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|2||0||0||2||0||8
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|2||0||0||2||1||8
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|8||1||1||6||6||30
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States of America}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|10||0||1||9||5||33
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!
!{{Tooltip|0/7|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!
!
!
!
!
!
!31
!2
!3
!26
!17
!119
|}
{{col-end}}

{{col-begin}}
{{col-2}}

===Olympic Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;font-size:90%;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the Summer Olympics|Olympic Games]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 0
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1896 Summer Olympics|1896]]&nbsp;– [[Football at the 1976 Summer Olympics|1976]]||colspan=8|''Part of {{flagIOC|FRA}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Soviet Union}} [[Football at the 1980 Summer Olympics|1980]]||colspan=8 rowspan=7|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[Football at the 1984 Summer Olympics|1984]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea}} [[Football at the 1988 Summer Olympics|1988]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[Football at the 1992 Summer Olympics|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[Football at the 1996 Summer Olympics|1996]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Australia}} [[Football at the 2000 Summer Olympics|2000]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Greece}} [[Football at the 2004 Summer Olympics|2004]]
|-
|{{flagicon|China}} [[Football at the 2008 Summer Olympics|2008]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|United Kingdom}} [[Football at the 2012 Summer Olympics|2012]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[Football at the 2016 Summer Olympics|2016]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Japan}} [[Football at the 2020 Summer Olympics|2020]]
|-
|{{flagicon|France}} [[Football at the 2024 Summer Olympics|2024]]
|-
!Total|| ||0/28|| || || || || ||
|}
*<small>Football at the Summer Olympics has been an under-23 tournament since the 1992 edition.</small>
{{col-end}}

{{col-begin}}
{{col-2}}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 0
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 Africa Cup of Nations|1957]] to {{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 Africa Cup of Nations|1976]]||colspan=8|''Part of {{fb|France|1974}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 Africa Cup of Nations|1978]] to {{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 Africa Cup of Nations|1992]]||colspan=8|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 Africa Cup of Nations|1994]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 Africa Cup of Nations|1996]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 Africa Cup of Nations|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 Africa Cup of Nations|2000]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mali}} [[2002 Africa Cup of Nations|2002]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia}} [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations|2004]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]||colspan=8 rowspan=6|''Did not qualify'' 
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
|-
!Total|| ||0/35|| || || || || ||
|}
{{col-2}}

===African Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[Football at the African Games|African Games]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 0
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Congo|1970}} [[Football at the 1965 All-Africa Games|1965]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Part of {{flagIOC|FRA}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[Football at the 1973 All-Africa Games|1973]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[Football at the 1978 All-Africa Games|1978]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[Football at the 1987 All-Africa Games|1987]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualification''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[Football at the 1991 All-Africa Games|1991]]||colspan=8 rowspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Zimbabwe}} [[Football at the 1995 All-Africa Games|1995]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[Football at the 1999 All-Africa Games|1999]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[Football at the 2003 All-Africa Games – Men's tournament|2003]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[Football at the 2007 All-Africa Games – Men's tournament|2007]]
|-
|{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[Football at the 2011 All-Africa Games – Men's tournament|2011]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Congo}} [[Football at the 2015 African Games – Men's tournament|2015]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[Football at the 2019 African Games – Men's tournament|2019]]
|-
!Total|| ||0/12|| || || || || ||
|}

* <small>Prior to the [[1991 All-Africa Games|Cairo 1991]] campaign, the [[Football at the All-Africa Games]] was open to full senior national teams.</small>
{{col-end}}

{{col-begin}}
{{col-2}}

===African Nations Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[African Nations Championship]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 0
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|CIV}} [[2009 African Nations Championship|2009]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|SUD}} [[2011 African Nations Championship|2011]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|RSA}} [[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|RWA}} [[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|MAR}} [[2018 African Nations Championship|2018]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|-
|{{flagicon|CMR}} [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]]||colspan=8|''Banned''
|-
|{{flagicon|ALG}} [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2024 African Nations Championship|2024]]
|-
!Total|| ||0/7|| || || || || ||
|}

{{col-2}}

===CECAFA Cup===
{{Main|CECAFA Cup}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[CECAFA Cup]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 10
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[1973 CECAFA Cup|1973]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Part of {{fb|France|1974}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[1974 CECAFA Cup|1974]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Zambia|1964}} [[1975 CECAFA Cup|1975]]
|-
|[[File:Flag of Tanzania.svg|border|22px|link=Zanzibar|Zanzibar]] [[1976 CECAFA Cup|1976]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Somalia}} [[1977 CECAFA Cup|1977]]||colspan=8 rowspan=15|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Malawi}} [[1978 CECAFA Cup|1978]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[1979 CECAFA Cup|1979]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan}} [[1980 CECAFA Cup|1980]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[1981 CECAFA Cup|1981]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[1982 CECAFA Cup|1982]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[1983 CECAFA Cup|1983]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[1984 CECAFA Cup|1984]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Zimbabwe}} [[1985 CECAFA Cup|1985]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1987}} [[1987 CECAFA Cup|1987]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Malawi}} [[1988 CECAFA Cup|1988]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[1989 CECAFA Cup|1989]]
|-
|[[File:Flag of Tanzania.svg|border|22px|link=Zanzibar|Zanzibar]] [[1990 CECAFA Cup|1990]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[1991 CECAFA Cup|1991]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[1992 CECAFA Cup|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[1994 CECAFA Cup|1994]]||Group Stage||8th||3||0||0||3||2||9
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[1995 CECAFA Cup|1995]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan}} [[1996 CECAFA Cup|1996]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda|1962}} [[1999 CECAFA Cup|1999]]||rowspan=3|Group Stage||11th||2||0||0||2||2||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2000 CECAFA Cup|2000]]||8th||4||0||1||3||4||15
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda}} [[2001 CECAFA Cup|2001]]||11th||3||0||0||3||3||17
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[2002 CECAFA Cup|2002]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan}} [[2003 CECAFA Cup|2003]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia}} [[2004 CECAFA Cup|2004]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda}} [[2005 CECAFA Cup|2005]]||rowspan=5|Group Stage||10th||4||0||0||4||2||18
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia}} [[2006 CECAFA Cup|2006]]||11th||3||0||0||3||0||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[2007 CECAFA Cup|2007]]||8th||3||0||0||3||2||19
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2008 CECAFA Cup|2008]]||10th||4||0||1||3||2||13
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[2009 CECAFA Cup|2009]]||12th||3||0||0||3||0||13
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[2010 CECAFA Cup|2010]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[2011 CECAFA Cup|2011]]||Group Stage||11th||3||0||0||3||2||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2012 CECAFA Cup|2012]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[2013 CECAFA Cup|2013]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda}} [[2015 CECAFA Cup|2015]]||Group Stage||11th||3||0||0||3||0||9
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[2017 CECAFA Cup|2017]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2019 CECAFA Cup|2019]]||Group Stage||6th||4||1||1||2||3||8
|-
!Total||0 Titles||12/39||35||1||3||35||22||147
|}
{{col-end}}

{{col-begin}}
{{col-2}}

===FIFA Arab Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[FIFA Arab Cup]] record
!width=1% rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|Arab Cup qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Lebanon}} [[1963 Arab Nations Cup|1963]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Part of {{fb|France|1974}}''||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Part of {{fb|France|1974}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Kuwait}} [[1964 Arab Nations Cup|1964]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Iraq|1963}} [[1966 Arab Nations Cup|1966]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[1985 Arab Nations Cup|1985]]||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not enter''||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Jordan}} [[1988 Arab Nations Cup|1988]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Syria}} [[1992 Arab Nations Cup|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[1998 Arab Nations Cup|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kuwait}} [[2002 Arab Nations Cup|2002]]
|-
|{{flagicon|}} ''[[2009 Arab Nations Cup|2009]]''||colspan=8|''Did not qualify'' {{Ref|n1|1}}
|2
|0
|0
|2
|3
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2012 Arab Nations Cup|2012]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2021 FIFA Arab Cup|2021]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
| 1
| 0
| 0
| 1
| 0
| 1
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2025 FIFA Arab Cup|2025]]
| 1
| 0
| 0
| 1
| 0
| 1
|-
!Total|| ||0/9|| || || || || ||
!4
!0
!0
!4
!3
!10
|}
{{note|n1|1}} <small>The [[2009 Arab Nations Cup|2009]] edition was cancelled during qualification.</small>

===Arab Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the Arab Games|Arab Games]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 0
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt|1922}} [[Football at the 1953 Arab Games|1953]]||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Part of {{flagIOC|FRA}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Lebanon}} [[Football at the 1957 Arab Games|1957]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[Football at the 1961 Arab Games|1961]]
|-
|{{flagicon|UAR}} [[Football at the 1965 Arab Games|1965]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Syria|1972}} [[Football at the 1976 Arab Games|1976]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[Football at the 1985 Arab Games|1985]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Syria}} [[Football at the 1992 Arab Games|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Lebanon}} [[Football at the 1997 Arab Games|1997]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Jordan}} [[Football at the 1999 Arab Games|1999]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} ''2004''||colspan=8|''No tournament''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[Football at the 2007 Arab Games|2007]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[Football at the 2011 Arab Games|2011]]
|-
!Total|| ||0/11|| || || || || || 
|}
{{col-end}}

==See also==
*[[French Somaliland national football team]]
*[[Djibouti national football team results]]
*[[Football in Djibouti]]
*[[Djiboutian Football Federation]]
*[[Djibouti Premier League]]
*[[Djibouti Cup]]
*[[Stade du Ville]]

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons category|Djibouti national association football team}}
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/dji/ Djibouti] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/DJI Djibouti] at [[FIFA]]
* [http://www.national-football-teams.com/v2/country.php?id=52 Djibouti] at National-Football-Teams.com.

{{Football in Djibouti}}
{{UAFA Football}}
{{CECAFA Football}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Djibouti}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Djibouti National Football Team}}
[[Category:Djibouti national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
